observations.recommendation: Trace the causes of the variances between the balances of the subsidiary ledgers and cashbooks so that necessary corrective actions/measures can be undertaken to reconcile the balances and present the correct balances.

A written explanation on charging the spare parts of the motor vehicle, repair and maintenance and wages of the JO Workers against the 20 per cent DF together with POW to establish the necessity of the expenditures to the PPAs under the 20 per cent DF; and

The required authority from the SB on the incurrence of expenditures charged against the PPAs in generic terms.

A written explanation on declaring the supplier as the winning bidder despite Red PhilGEPS Certificate; and 



The General and Special Conditions of the Contract of the above-mentioned infrastructure projects for the Audit Team’s evaluation.

Submit the certification that the medicines and drugs requisitioned is included in the PNDF Current Edition and require the supplier to furnish details such as Lot Nos. and expiry dates on the procured medicines for the procurement made on October 23, 2023 under Check No. 89327535; and

Submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) as supporting documents of the recorded expenses and prepare journal entries, if necessary, as well as record subsequent purchases of inventories in accordance with Section 51 of the Manual on the NGAS.

Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to the DO for reimbursement of travel expenses of other officials and employees, instead the regular procedures in payment be implemented to ensure the regularity of the expenditures before the disbursements.

Three Department Heads plantilla positions are not filled in for more than two years despite the availability of appropriation inconsistent with Sections 76 and 444(b)(1)(v) of Republic Act No. 7160 and Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circular No. 24, Series of 2017, which could affect the effectiveness and efficiency of the Municipality’s operation.

Trace the causes of the under-remittances so that necessary actions/measures can be undertaken such as immediate remittance of taxes and/or adjustments in the books of accounts, whichever is applicable.

Trace, in coordination with the Human Resource Management Office, the causes of the under-remittance of the contributions and loan payments on GSIS and Pag-IBIG and the under deduction of contributions on Philhealth premiums so that necessary actions/measures can be undertaken; and

Correct the erroneous recording of payment to Cebu CFI Community Cooperative with following proposed adjusting journal entry:

Dr:  Other Payable ₱100,777.87
Cr:   Due to GSIS  ₱100,777.87
To correct erroneous recording on the remittance of the loan payment to Cebu CFI Community Cooperative
To update the RPTAR to be able to generate the appropriate amount to be set-up as RPT and SET receivables.
Designate personnel or create a committee who will facilitate the process to intensify/expedite the titling efforts of the Municipality.
Submit justification on the price difference mentioned above, since no explanation has been submitted, despite the Audit Team’s request through email, to enable us to make a decision in audit.

The Chief Executive and the Bids and Awards Committee submit an explanation on why the Lowest Calculated Bid passed the criteria on post-qualification despite the inconsistencies in the NFCC computation and other deficiencies noted.

The Accounting unit to file anew the request for authority to write-off for dormant receivable accounts pursuant to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 and to submit supporting schedules containing the required details to facilitate review of items in the schedule.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the concerned departments responsible in the recording of the road network of the Municipality to coordinate and establish a system to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements under COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
Inquire from the DILG Regional Office VII for the training schedules on LRAMs, and allow the concerned officials to attend, if available.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Inventory Committee spearheaded by the Property Officer to expedite the conduct the physical count of PPE as of December 31, 2024, render the report thereon and provide a copy of the report to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.
Also, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and/or Property and Supplier Officer to maintain property cards for monitoring and reconciliation.

We recommended and Management agreed to designate personnel or create a committee who will expedite the titling efforts of the Municipality. Also, seek legal assistance from Provincial Legal Office relative to acquiring absolute ownership of the lands especially those discrepancies between the actual occupants and the declared owner so that proper action can be taken.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that projects, programs and activities in the Annual Invest Plan for 20 per cent development fund are specific and implementation-ready. Otherwise, an SB Resolution be first obtained which serve as an authority of the local chief executive to enter into a contract for the implementation of PPAs expressed in generic terms.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the MDRRMO to submit the following:
Amended LDRRMFIP that includes the list of projects and activities charged against the unexpended LDRRMF in the previous years in conformity with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and henceforth, observe the timely submission thereof.

8.7 We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Treasurer, to release the RPT share to Barangays, on a quarterly basis, pursuant to Section 271 (d) of RA 7160 in order for the latter to utilize the funds for its priority projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the Municipal Engineer, to ensure that the Certificate of Final Acceptance be issued only after the lapsed of the defects liability period, which is one-year from the date of project completion in order to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the HRMO intensify its hiring process in order to  fill-up the vacant plantilla position to enhance its services and to attain full implementation of programs, projects and activities of the municipality.

We recommended and the BAC agreed, through the BAC Secretariat, to observe the deadline set by the GPPB on the submission of approved APP, PMR and APCPI to the GPPB pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 to increase and enhance the transparency on Municipality’s procurement transactions.

Reconcile the balances of the unspent LDRRM Fund of the current year and make necessary adjustments, if necessary.

Make necessary adjusting entry to revert back the unspent CO of the 70 per cent Mitigation Fund to the General Fund and remain continuing until completion of the projects/programs, with the proposed adjusting journal entry:

General Fund Books
Dr: Due to Other Funds 		₱245,316.50
Cr: Transfer Of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds To The Trust Fund     ₱245,316.50

To revert the capital outlay portion of the unspent LDRRM of the current year to the General Fund as continuing appropriation. 

Trust Fund Books
Dr: Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund			          ₱245,316.50
 
Cr: Due from Other Funds	     ₱245,316.50

To revert the capital outlay portion of the unspent LDRRM of the current year to the General Fund as continuing appropriation.
Prioritize the implementation of all PPAs intended for LDRRM which are the means to prevent, prepare and mitigate the risks and the adverse effects of calamities.

Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer to determine unappropriated budget/savings in the General Fund, where the expenses can be charged and to replenish the DRRMF; and

Give priorities to the PPAs enumerated in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2013-1 and other pertinent rules and regulations, and to use the fund according to its intended purpose.

Hired qualified personnel once financial resources allow to occupy the position of three (3) DRRM Staff to complement the Local DRRM Officer and properly discharge the functions and responsibilities of the LDRRM office in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2022.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and
the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances in their respective records,
identify the items that need adjustments and record them appropriately in the
affected books, and to perform the same regularly pursuant to Section 63 (F) of
BLGF-DOF LTOM, to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

We reiterated the recommendation and Management agreed to:
 commence the process of disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance
with the guidelines set forth in COA No. 89-296 and make sure that the
properties presented under PPE are only those assets used in the operation of
the LGU;
 direct the custodian of one computer set to file a request for relief of
accountability so that appropriate action will be undertaken;
 instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remove in the inventory record the items
with decision of relief of property accountability.
 request for authority from COA for the derecognition of the properties which
records are not available by following the procedures and complying with the
documentary requirements under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 13,
2020 on the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to
conduct physical count of inventory items by type and reported in the RPCI to be
submitted to the Audit Team. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to conduct reconciliation on its balances periodically to be able
to produce reliable financial reports.

We recommended that the Management:
 Submit justification on the price difference mentioned above, since no
explanation has been submitted, despite the Audit Team’s request through
email, to enable us to make a decision in audit.
 Meticulously plan the procurement and conduct market survey on the current
market price of the goods to be procured to avoid the setting of an exorbitant
approved budget for the contract, which may lead to an overprice transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the Municipal
Development Council, submit an explanation/justification for the non-
implementation of these priority projects. We also recommended Management to
always ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally
utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the
disposition of government resources in compliance with the provisions set forth in
DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1.

We recommended that Management refrain from appropriating in the Annual Budget
the payment of honoraria intended for the BAC, instead, assess its utilization to
determine if there are savings that can supplement the deficiency from the
collections mentioned above. Also, submit the documentary requirements
necessary in the payment of said expenditure, for the Audit Team’s verification, so
as to be able to make a decision in audit.

We recommended that the Management:
 Submit justification with supporting documents on the grant of honoraria to
the Engineer I, who was designated as Municipal Budget Officer, and to the
Supply Officer, who acted as concurrent Disbursing Officer, which is in
excess of the rate authorized by law and without legal basis, respectively;
 Exert more effort in filling in the vacant plantilla positions especially the
Municipal Budget Officer position and if possible, coordinate with the Civil
Service Commission on the advertisement of the vacant positions of the LGU
to increase the probability of hiring qualified candidates; and
 Refrain from granting honoraria to permanent employees, who are
designated in concurrent position that is beyond the rate authorized by law.

We recommended that Management:
 Limit the grant of monetization of leave credits to officials and employees who
meet the required number of leave credits set forth by CSC;
 Utilize CSC Form 6: Application for leave form upon availing of leaves and/or
monetization of leave duly approved by the head of office. Duplicate copy will
be kept in the HRMO file while the triplicate copy will be attached to the DV;
 Instruct the HR Officer to:
 Prepare Summary of Leave Credit balances of all employees at the end of the
year duly attested by the employees of their balances; and
 Determine first the balance of employee’s leave credits before
granting/approving the application for monetization.

We recommended and Management agreed to observe the procedural guidelines of
MNGAS in the disbursements of government funds and prepare the Disbursement
Voucher for every payment including those made through the ATM account of the
payee.
We recommended that Management direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit the liquidation reports of cash advances for ESP and for other purposes due for January to December 2020.
observations.managementAction: The liquidation reports of cash advances for ESP and for other purposes due for January to December 2020 were submitted on February 26, 2021.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 was complied. reports were submitted on February 26, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management issue guidelines on implementation to standardize rates of assistance to be extended by the Municipality, through the MSWDO, to victims of disaster/s, displaced and distressed individuals, families and communities in crisis situation and to serve as a guide in determining the amount of assistance to be granted based on the degree of need as well as the existing resources of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: On March 4, 2021 Management submitted a copy of the implementing guidelines on the Provision of Minimum Standard Rates of Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situation (AICS) including the Assistance to Victims of Disasters (Natural and Man-made), Distressed and Displaced Individuals (Victims of Armed Conflict) and Families in Crisis Situation drafted by the concerned Chief Executive for the first deliberation for approval on February 24, 2021. Furthermore, the MSWDO was informed to take on the implementation. The said guidelines were enacted and approved on March 8, 2021. Resolution No. 022-2021 Series of 2021 adopting the Implementing guidelines on the Provision of Minimum Standard Rates of Assistance to individuals in crisis situation (AICS) including the Assistance to Victims of Disasters (natural and man-made), distressed and displaced individuals (victims of armed conflict) and families in crisis situation was enacted and approved on March 8, 2021.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 Management submitted copy of the implementing guidelines on the Implementation of AICS. Henceforth implementation was enacted and approved on March 8, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit an explanation (emergency cases) together with the revised Program of Work, Purchase Request, Abstract of Canvass, Requisition and Issue Slip and Acceptance and Inspection Report.
observations.managementAction: On March 4, 2021, Management submitted a negotiated explanation together with the revised Program of Work pursuant to Section 53 of RA 9184 and Resolution No. 03-2020 for a transaction intended for the regular operations of the Municipality. The revised Program of Work, Purchase Request, Abstract of Canvass, Requisition and Issue Slip and Inspection and Acceptance Report were submitted.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 The recommendation was complied. Submitted documents indicated transaction intended for regular operations and supporting documents were provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management secure valid official receipts/invoices from the supplier to properly substantiate the payments and an explanation from the supplier on why receipts were issued.
observations.managementAction: Three valid official receipts were submitted.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 The following official receipts were received. The supplier issued valid receipts after request and explanation was provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit written justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act and ensure compliance with the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and always updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.
observations.managementAction: Submitted a justification. On March 4, 2021, the LGU submitted a written explanation. The explanation provided that goods procured were immediately delivered/received and OSS with the LGU. The explanation stated that goods procured were immediately dispensed to the people once received by the LGU and the warranty clause requirement under regular procurement was not practicable under emergency procurement.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 Submitted a justification. The explanation was found acceptable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chief Executive and the Bids and Awards Committee submit an explanation on the biological transformation of breeding stocks and a reconciliation of the changes in the carrying amount between the beginning and the end of CY 2020 and include a disclosure in the notes to the financial statements to convey information on the biological changes.
observations.managementAction: On January 8, 2021 the Municipal Accountant submitted the schedules and ledgers that contain the details of the receivables items and provided the revised Report on the Biological Asset Card send through email on May 26, 2021. The Municipal Government submitted supporting documentation on the swine dispersal report 2020 & Biological Asset Card.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 Submitted documents on the changes in carrying amount and reconciliation. The revised notes to financial statements included disclosure on changes in the carrying amount of the biological assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management include the Reports of Checks Issued and cancelled checks for evaluation and submit the original copies of the cancelled checks for custody and validation.
observations.managementAction: The original copies of the cancelled checks from January to December 2020 were submitted altogether on February 26, 2021. Further, it was recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile their records, and adherence to the provisions of the Government Accounting Manual and other prescribed regulations is enjoined to which they also agreed.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 The original copies of the cancelled checks were submitted on March 4, 2021 and reconciliation was recommended and agreed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with Section 8 of RA 11313 and Section 9 of its IRR for the enforcement of the Safe Spaces Act and pass an ordinance localizing the applicability of the law.
observations.managementAction: Management wrote in their reply received by the team on January 8, 2021 that they are now taking actions to comply with Section 8 of RA 11313 and Section 9 of its IRR for the enforcement. First reading by the Sangguniang Bayan Members was made on January 6, 2021. The Recommendation further indicated that passage of an ordinance localizing the applicability of the law shall follow after the third reading principle. Resolution and Ordinance actions followed and Ordinance No. 2021-04 Series of 2021, an Ordinance Localizing the Applicability of RA No. 11313 was passed on May 28, 2021.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 Management submitted written reply and subsequently enacted Ordinance No. 2021-04 Series of 2021 on May 28, 2021 localizing the law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Petty Cash Custodian to limit the charges against the PC to those non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses pursuant to Section 84 of the GAM for LGUs. A separate cash advance may be drawn for other current operating expenses when it is impractical to pay the same by check.
observations.managementAction: A separate cash advance for other current operating expenses will be drawn for those purposes in the ensuing year. Copies of the CAFOA, the designation and bond application of the Petty Cash Custodian were also submitted.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 Compliance was observed as liquidation of cash advances for travelling expenses and financial assistance were drawn as cash advance for other current operating expenses and documentation was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create a special account for BGCM in accordance with LBC No. 125 to ensure transparency and adequacy of financial information on the receipt and utilization of BGCM.
observations.managementAction: The BGCM was fully utilized. The LGU created and maintained required records in accordance with LBC No. 125 and documents were submitted to the Audit Team on March 4, 2021.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 The BGCM account was established and full documentation submitted on March 4, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management make representation with LBP/DBP to receive the bank statements in a timely manner or consider the other options in order to submit the BRS on time to facilitate the audit. We also recommended that Management provide an explanation on the unlabeled reconciling item presented in the LBP General Fund account BRS.
observations.managementAction: Compliance was emailed to the Audit Team Leader, Ms. Rhoda Morales on Feb. 26, 2021 informing that the 4th quarter BRS would be submitted. The 4th quarter BRS was submitted on March 7, 2022.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 The 4th quarter BRS submission was eventually made; management provided representation and the BRS was submitted on March 7, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventory items by type as of December 31, 2020, reconcile with accounting records, render the report thereon and provide a copy of the report to the audit team pursuant to Section 194 of the GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Compliance was submitted and stamped received by COA on Mar. 4, 2021.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 The submitted report for CY 2020 showed discrepancy between quantities per count and per card. The observation is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit an explanation/justification on the non-posting of the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 01-2020. Henceforth, the BAC be updated in the GPPB online portal and visit the GPPB website regularly to avoid non-compliance in future.
observations.managementAction: Submitted a written explanation. Verification with the LGU website revealed that items procured through emergency purchases were posted at conspicuous places and in the LGU website. The LGU has no procurement thru the emergency purchases in CY 2021.
observations.reason: AAR 2020 The LGU explained the procurements were posted in conspicuous places and the LGU website; however, posting in the GPPB online portal was not consistently done. The LGU committed to comply going forward.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management commence the process of disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are only those assets used in the operation of the LGU; determine the persons liable for unaccounted properties for appropriate legal action; intensify efforts in determining the composition of the properties without details and effect the proper classification of accounts to present more accurately the balances of PPE in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management wrote that the LGU is contemplating the disposal/divestment of unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 79 of the PD No. 1445. The revised Report on the PPE as of December 31, 2019 under SEF included the details of unaccounted properties. The small items may be monitored through the issuance of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS). Schedule of Other Machinery and Equipment was also provided. JEV No. 100-19-12-212 was issued under the GF to take up the small items included in Other Machinery and Equipment account. The management submitted documents indicating amounts and adjustments.
observations.reason: AAR 2019 The JEV was issued to take up small items; the revised report included details; however, there was no complete action by management with regard to full disposal of unserviceable items. Observation reiterated in Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chief Executive and the Bids and Awards Committee submit an explanation on why the Lowest Calculated Bid passed the post-qualification despite the inconsistencies in the NFCC computation and other deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: On January 8, 2021, the LGU submitted a written reply wherein the Management justified that the BAC hardly cope up with the procedures since the majority procurements were done thru the Emergency Purchases. The Training Institute will submit a committed line of credit in place of the inconsistent NFCC. The Training Institute also decided to replace the inconsistent NFCC with evidence of payment. The submitted documents were found to be in order, however, copies of the committed line of credit and Official Receipt from the Training Institute are still not received.
observations.reason: AAR 2019 Documents on deficiencies were submitted and were found to be in order except that copies of the committed line of credit and Official Receipt from the Training Institute are still not received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit an explanation on what transpired during the process and make representations to the DSWD as to the proper action to be done, send demand letters to the beneficiaries for the refund of the amount received totaling ₱1,332,000.00, ensure collection and return the same to DSWD.
observations.managementAction: On March 4, 2021, Management submitted a written reply signed by the concerned Chief Executive explaining that the submitted list of ineligible beneficiaries was disqualified during the 2nd tranche pay-out implemented by the LGU. There was a misunderstanding in sending the list. The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office did their best to distribute the funds to the less privileged families who were most affected during the pandemic. Further, a demand and collection action was recommended and the team was waiting for confirmation from DSWD.
observations.reason: AAR 2019 The LGU submitted explanation and claimed efforts to distribute funds to deserving beneficiaries; however, confirmation from DSWD and collection status of funds from ineligible beneficiaries remain pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management submit justification letter supported with copies of the variation order issued by the LGU directing the contractor to amend plans and specification and revised Statement of Work Accomplishment (SWA) to COA to correct the deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: Required documentation and other deficiencies to avoid disallowance were submitted on March 24, 2020. The LGU indicated that a counter offer was accepted in lieu of liquidated damages since the contractor made additional works that were not part of the original program of works. The Municipality accepted the counter offer; hence liquidated damages were not imposed.
observations.reason: AAR 2019 The submitted documents were in order; however, justification that a counter offer was accepted in lieu of liquidated damages was provided. The liquidated damages could not be validated as the activity or add-ons were not part of the original program of work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting unit file anew the request for authority to write-off for dormant receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 and submit supporting schedules containing the required details to validate the claim; record the provision of Impairment Loss and corresponding Allowance for Impairment to set up provision for losses arising from non-collection or non-liquidation of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the schedules and ledgers that contain the details of the receivables as well as adjusting entries to effect the recommendation on the provision of impairment and corresponding allowance for impairment. Request for write-off was filed on March 25, 2020 but supporting documents such as certification by the responsible officials that there are no records/documents available to validate the claim and it includes receivables related to cash shortages which is not covered with COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.reason: AAR 2019 Schedules and ledgers were submitted and request for write-off filed, but some supporting documents and certifications required under COA Circular 2016-005 were not provided; provision for impairment entries were being considered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management hire eventually qualified personnel once financial resources allow to occupy the position of the three (3) DRRM Staff to complement the Local DRRM Officer and properly discharge the functions and responsibilities of the LDRRM office in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The LGU hired the DRRM officer supported by three permanent personnel to perform the tasks of DRRM staff. The functions of three mandated positions are performed by designated personnel. The LGU indicated that the three DRRM staff cannot be employed yet due to budget constraints.
observations.reason: AAR 2017 The DRRM officer was hired but the three mandated DRRM staff positions could not be filled due to budget constraints; functions are being performed by designated personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management commence the process of disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are only those assets used in the operation of the LGU.

It was also recommended that concerned officials/employees intensify efforts in determining the composition of the properties without details, and adherence to the accounting policies on loss of properties provided in Section 268 of the GAM for LGUs is enjoined.

We recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventory items by type as of December 31, 2020, reconcile with accounting records, render the report thereon and provide a copy of the report to the audit team pursuant to Section 194 of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include a disclosure in the financial statements to convey information on the biological transformation of breeding stocks and a reconciliation of the changes in the carrying amount between the beginning and the end of CY 2020.

We recommended that Management make representation with LBP/DBP to receive the bank statements in a timely manner or consider the other options in order to submit the BRS on time to facilitate the audit.  We also recommended that Management provide an explanation on the unlabeled reconciling item presented in the LBP General Fund account BRS.

We recommended that Management direct the Petty Cash Custodian to limit the charges against the PC to those non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses pursuant to Section 84 of the GAM for LGUs.   A separate cash advance may be drawn for other current operating expenses when it is impractical to pay the same by check.
We recommended that Management direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to submit the liquidation reports of cash advances for ESP and for other purposes due for submission.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the original copies of the Reports of Checks Issued and cancelled checks for evaluation by this office.  Further, it was recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile their records, and adherence to the provisions of the Government Accounting Manual and other prescribed regulations is enjoined to which they also agreed.
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor as well as the Municipal Accountant agreed to create a special account for BGCM in accordance with LBC No. 125.

We recommended that the Chief Executive and the Bids and Awards Committee submit an explanation on why the Lowest Calculated Bid passed the criteria on post-qualification despite the inconsistencies in the NFCC computation and other deficiencies noted.

We recommended that Management issue guidelines on the AICS implementation to standardize rates of assistance to be extended by the Municipality, through the MSWDO, to the victims of disaster/s, displaced and distressed individuals, groups, families and communities in crisis situation and to serve as a guide in determining the amount of assistance to be granted based on the degree of need as well as the existing resources of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation on the employment of negotiated procurement (emergency cases) modality pursuant to Section 53 of RA 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020 for a transaction intended for the regular operations of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit an explanation/justification on the non-compliance with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.

Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC be updated always of the GPPB issuances and advisories by visiting their website regularly to avoid non-compliance in the future.
We recommended that Management secure valid official receipts/invoices from the supplier to properly substantiate payments and an explanation from the supplier on why invalid receipts were issued.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act without complying with the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and to be always updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation on what have transpired during the process and make representation to the DSWD as to the proper action to be taken on this matter.  Furthermore, we recommended that Management send demand letters to the 222 ineligible beneficiaries for the refund of the amount received totaling ₱1,332,000.00, ensure the collection thereof and return the same to DSWD.
We recommended that Management comply with Section 8 of RA 11313 and Section 9 of its IRR to ensure enforcement.
Management direct the Accountant to record properly the retention money to present fairly the accounts in the financial statements at any reporting dates.
observations.managementAction: The retention money were already released to contractors substituted with surety bonds with proper entries recorded as shown in the submitted ledgers and index payment cards.

observations.recommendation: Management direct the concerned officials/employees to submit an explanation on the continued disregard of the previous years’ recommendation as well as the required documents for the afore-cited transactions including the supplemental APP.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was complied.

observations.recommendation: The concerned Chief Executive direct Municipal Accountant to submit immediately subject records, disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports due for submission.  It is further recommended that payment of salaries be suspended for those officials/employees who failed to submit reports within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was complied.

observations.recommendation: Management submit a written explanation on why the Annual Procurement Plan for 2019 is not consistent with the duly approved annual budget for the same period.  Henceforth, preparation of APP should be consistent with the annual budget and in accordance with RA No. 9184 and related issuances.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was complied.

observations.recommendation:
Management establish an Internal Audit Service Office, to improve the collaboration and confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445. 

We also recommended that Management install a sound system of internal control to strengthen accountability, ensure ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations, to improve the quality and quantity of outputs and to better respond to the needs of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials explained that a separate and independent Internal Audit Service Office has not been established due to restrictions on PS limitation. Other positions of equal importance such as the MENRO, PESO, Information Officer, Tourism Officer have not yet been created.

observations.recommendation:
Management commence the process of disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are only those assets used in the operation of the LGU as well as recognize in the books the unrecorded kitchen equipment.  Small items may be monitored through the issuance of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS).  

It is also recommended that concerned officials/employees intensify efforts in determining the composition of the properties without details and effect the proper classification of accounts to present more accurately the balances of PPE in the financial statements.  The persons liable for unaccounted properties should be determined for appropriate legal action.
observations.managementAction:
The management through its written reply received last March 24, 2020 expressed that the LGU is contemplating on the disposal/divestment of unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 79 of the PD No. 1445.  The revised Report on the PPE as of December 31, 2019 under SEF already included the details of unaccounted properties.
	
As for the kitchen equipment, the same were already recorded in the account Other Machinery and Equipment (1-07-05-990) SL-21 in the total amount of ₱842,940.95. Schedule of Other Machinery and Equipment was also provided.
observations.reason:
The small items totaling ₱163,106.35, JEV No. 100-19-12-212 was issued under the GF to take up the small items included in the Other Machinery and Equipment account.

There was no action by management with regard to the unserviceable.

observations.recommendation: Management submit the required documentation and justify other noted deficiencies to avoid disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Required documentation were submitted on March 24, 2020.
observations.reason: Liquidated damages were not imposed since the contractor made an additional works as recommended by the DILG Engineer that were not part originally to the program of works.  Instead of liquidated damages, a counter offer was accepted by the Municipality of Ginatilan, Cebu. | The explanation of management that a counter offer was accepted in lieu of the liquidated damages cannot be accepted.  As pointed out, the activity or add-ons were not part of the original program of work, thus cannot be validated.

observations.recommendation: Management direct the Accounting unit to file anew the request for authority to write-off for dormant receivable accounts pursuant to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 and to submit supporting schedules containing the required details to facilitate review of items in the schedule. It is also recommended that the Accountant consider recording the provision of Impairment Loss and corresponding Allowance for Impairment to set up provision for losses arising from non-collection or non-liquidation of receivables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the schedules and ledgers that contain the details of the receivables as well as adjusting entries to effect the recommendation on the provision of impairment loss and corresponding allowance for impairment.
observations.reason: Request for authority to write-off was still not refiled to conform to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: Management eventually hire qualified personnel once financial resources allow to occupy the position of three (3) DRRM Staff to complement the Local DRRM Officer and properly discharge the functions and responsibilities of the LDRRM office in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The LGU hired the DRRM Officer supported by three permanent personnel designated to perform the tasks of DRRM staff supported by Emergency Responders.
observations.reason:
The DRRM officer was already hired but the three DRRM staff cannot be employed yet due to budget constraints.

The functions of three mandated positions are performed by designated personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Accounting unit to file anew the request for authority to write-off for dormant receivable accounts pursuant to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 and to submit supporting schedules containing the required details to facilitate review of items in the schedule. It is also recommended that the Accountant consider recording the provision of Impairment Loss and corresponding Allowance for Impairment to set up provision for losses arising from non-collection or non-liquidation of receivables.

We recommended that management commence the process of disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are only those assets used in the operation of the LGU as well as recognize in the books the unrecorded kitchen equipment.  Small items may be monitored through the issuance of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS).  The following entry is proposed:

Under the books of the GF

It is also recommended that concerned officials/employees intensify efforts in determining the composition of the properties without details and effect the proper classification of accounts to present more accurately the balances of PPE in the financial statements.  The persons liable for unaccounted properties should be determined for appropriate legal action.
We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to record properly the retention money to present fairly the accounts in the financial statements at any reporting dates.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned officials/employees to submit an explanation on the continued disregard of the previous years’ recommendation as well as the required documents for the afore-cited transactions including the supplemental APP.
We recommended that Management submit the required documentation and justify other noted deficiencies to avoid disallowance.

We recommended that the concerned Chief Executive direct Municipal Accountant to submit immediately subject records, disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports due for submission.  It is further recommended that payment of salaries be suspended for those officials/employees who failed to submit reports within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 122 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management establish an Internal Audit Service Office, to improve the collaboration and confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of PD 1445.

We also recommended that Management install a sound system of internal control to strengthen accountability, ensure ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations, to improve the quality and quantity of outputs and to better respond to the needs of the constituents.

We recommended that management submit a written explanation on why the Annual Procurement Plan for 2019 is not consistent with the duly approved annual budget for the same period.  Henceforth, preparation of APP should be consistent with the annual budget and in accordance with RA No. 9184 and related issuances.
Management direct concerned officials/employees to submit the required documents to avoid disallowance in audit.

Municipal Accounting Office be directed to determine if all these checks were indeed released to the proper payees.  Confirmation and/or notices should be sent to the respective payees/creditors so as to verify the receipt of said checks and to inquire as to why these have not been presented to the bank for payment.  

Also, have stale checks still in the hands of the Treasurer, cancelled and replaced according to the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and coordinate with the Accounting Office for proper recording in the books.  Stale checks that are already released to the payees shall only be cancelled and replaced upon presentation of the stale checks.  Henceforth, the Office of the Municipal Treasurer should send a written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding check.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Journal entry vouchers numbered 100-2018-01-001, 200-2018-01-002 and 300-2018-01-001 all dated January 31, 2018 were prepared by the Accounting unit to effect the adjustments.

observations.recommendation: Management submit evidences that procedural requirements for above negotiated procurement were indeed conducted.  Otherwise, submit a valid explanation on the non-conduct of such procedural requirements to avoid disallowance in audit.

Should there have lapses it might be due to minimal inadvertence or the fact that the RA 9184 are usually amended, which were not timely relayed to them.
observations.reason: Written reply together with the evidences of posting in the PhilGEPS website were received on March 2, 2018.  For 2018 procurement, posting of RFQs in the PhilGEPS website were already done.

We recommend that the Municipal Accounting Office be directed to
determine if all these checks were indeed released to the proper payees.
Confirmation and/or notices should be sent to the respective payees/creditors so as
to verify the receipt of said checks and to inquire as to why these have not been
presented to the bank for payment.

Also, have stale checks still in the hands of the Treasurer, cancelled and
replaced according to the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of NGAS Manual,
Volume I and coordinate with the Accounting Office for proper recording in the
books. Stale checks that are already released to the payees shall only be cancelled
and replaced upon presentation of the stale checks. Henceforth, the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer should send a written notice to the payee at least one month
before a check becomes stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding
check.

We recommend that management commence the process of disposal of
unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA
Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are
only those assets used in the operation of the LGU.

1) file the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated fund
transfers pursuant to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-
005;
2) return the unspent PDAF funds to the National Treasury so that it can
be used for the other priority projects of the government; and
3) conduct regular monitoring and analysis to ensure that fund transfers
are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature
and purpose.

We recommend that management direct those concerned employees to
exercise due care and diligence in the inspection, evaluation and acceptance of
projects turned over by the contractor and require the contractor to institute
appropriate corrections on the stated deficiencies pursuant to Section 62 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

In view of the above deficiencies, we recommend that management submit
evidences that procedural requirements for above negotiated procurement were
indeed conducted. Otherwise, submit a valid explanation on the non-conduct of
such procedural requirements to avoid disallowance in audit.

We, therefore, recommend that management cause the refund of undue
payments of clothing allowance made to job order workers. Henceforth, refrain
from granting allowances/benefits enjoyed by government employees to job order
workers.

justification on why the payment of clothing allowance to job order workers was
charged against Repairs and Maintenance-Buildings and Other Structures and
effect the necessary adjustments for proper classification of accounts to present
more accurately the balances of accounts in the financial statements.

strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities advocating
child rights separate from its allocation for other social services and use the fund
pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

responsibilities of the LDRRM office in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2014-1
dated April 4, 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that copies of contract documents shall be furnished to the Auditor not later than five days from perfection and duly accomplished supporting documents shall be required before payment to establish the validity of the claims. observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Accountant to record tires purchased for operations as inventory account and to credit the same for issuance to end users.

She, however, added that in CY 2016, purchase of tires were still not recorded as inventories.
observations.reason: Unspent items were recorded as expenses; in CY 2016 purchases of tires were still not recorded as inventories.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare LDRRMFIP in accordance with Annex “A” of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and submit Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant to the concerned Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Further, comply with other requirements under RA No. 10121 and follow the guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the accounting and reporting of its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
observations.managementAction: Timely submission of LDRRMF reports was not done in 2016.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, timely submission of LDRRMF reports was still not done in 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management create an inventory committee to conduct
the physical count of PPE and shall prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment for submission to the concerned auditor.

We recommend that the concerned chief executive cause the immediate
submission of said BRS and institute appropriate sanctions on employees
responsible for the delayed submission/non-submission, if warranted on the basis of
the evidences/documents.

We recommend that the concerned Local Chief Executive cause the
immediate submission of the subject disbursement vouchers, supporting documents
and other reports due for submission to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommend that management cause the submission of documents
evidencing extension of time, if any, other required documents and/or an
explanation on why the contractor was allowed to commence work of said project
eight months after it was supposed to start.

We recommend that management submit the above-specified documents or a
written explanation why the same could not be submitted.

We recommend that management submit the MOA between the LGU and
DOLE for the receipt of funds, Trust Agreement/MOA between the LGU and the
beneficiary association, project proposal or application for funding, accreditation
documents and other supporting documents to substantiate the receipt of funds and
implementation of said livelihood project.

We, therefore, recommend that the Municipal Accountant affix her
signature/certification on the computation of the HRMO of the terminal leave
benefits of officials and employees to signify review made by the Accounting Unit
and that claims for terminal leave benefits be supported with the complete
documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that management comply with the pertinent provisions of
COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and 96-010 dated August 15,
1996 on the prompt submission of contracts and supporting documents, purchase
orders, work orders and notices of deliveries to ensure timely evaluation and
verification thereof.

We recommend that the municipality should prepare and submit/furnish a
copy of monthly reports in the prescribed forms required under COA Circular Nos.
2012-002 and 2014-002 to the National DRRM Council through the Office of the
Civil Defense (OCD), and to the COA Auditor.

We recommend that management direct the Accountant to submit the subsidiary schedules supporting the thirty-one (31) general ledger account balances. Henceforth, maintain subsidiary ledgers and records of accounts duly supported with complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR Subsidiary 2015 schedules are already attached to the year-end trial balances. Henceforth, maintain subsidiary ledgers and records of accounts duly supported with complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The 2016 trial balances are already attached to the year-end trial balances are already attached already attached to the year-end complete the review on the accuracy of the ledger account balances. schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer reflect the difference on the reported cash tickets issued in the amount of P8,355.00 as against the computation of the same in the Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms with Money Value.
observations.managementAction: AAR The amount of 2015 P8,355.00 was collected from accountable officer and deposited in the LGU’s depository account.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer had recorded in the collections of authorized P8,355.00 and deposited in the depository bank. The amount was deposited in the LGU’s depository account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments to present more accurately the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR Adjustments were already made by the Accountant. A journal entry was prepared by the Municipal Accountant in December 2015 to reclassify Construction in Progress-Infrastructure Assets of P704,591.00 to Repairs and Maintenance-Construction and Heavy Equipment.
observations.reason: Reclassification was done. The 2015 adjustments are already attached to the payment voucher and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: 1) Deduct from project cost the amount of P6,102.00 and direct those concerned employees to exercise due care and diligence in the inspection, evaluation and subsequent acceptance of projects from the contractor; 2) Cause the submission of the following documents: a) Eligibility requirements under Section 23.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. b) Printout copy of posting of Notice to Proceed and Contract of Award in the PhilGEPS. c) Approved Budget for the Contract. d) Bid Evaluation Report. e) Sworn statement by the prospective bidder or its duly authorized representative in the form prescribed by the GPPB as to the following: i. It is not “blacklisted” or barred from bidding by the GOP or any of its agencies, offices, corporations, or LGUs, including foreign government/foreign or international financing institution whose blacklisting rules have been recognized by the GPPB; ii. Each of the documents submitted in satisfaction of the bidding requirements is an authentic copy of the original, complete, and all statements and information provided therein are true and correct; iii. It is authorizing the Head of the Procuring Entity or his duly authorized representative/s to verify all the documents submitted; and iv. It complies with the disclosure provision under Section 47 of RA 9184 in relation to other provisions of RA 3019. f) Evidence of Invitation of one more observer in all stages of the procurement process. (Copies of invitation for COA and the President of GMFSC are already attached.)
observations.managementAction: Documents for submission were attached in the first payment voucher, and another copy of them were given on October 13, 2015. However, the agency apologized that posting of Notice to Proceed in the PhilGEPS started only in CY 2016.
observations.reason: The amount was deducted from the final payment to the Contractor. Final payment to the contractor was deducted the amount of P6,102.00 for the non-compliance with safety requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Accountant to record medical supplies, drugs and medicines purchased for operations as inventory accounts and to credit the same for issuance to end-users, transfers or other disposal using the appropriate accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of medical supplies, drugs and medicines are already recorded as inventories and medicines that are already recorded as inventories and issuances thereof were recorded as inventories.
observations.reason: Verification of accounts in CY 2016 disclosed that purchases of medical supplies, drugs and medicines are already recorded as inventories. Issuances thereof were recorded as inventories and supported with RIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the specified documents to establish the validity of the claims.
observations.managementAction: Required documents such as the appointment of the OSCA head as Chairman of Senior Citizen’s Affairs by the agency head, authority to collect honoraria, plan/program of activities for senior citizens, accomplishment reports/certificate of completion of programmed activities, etc which we found in order were received.
observations.reason: This office had received the required documents on March 1, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate the Local GAD Code and ensure that Executive Legislative Agenda (ELA), Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP), Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) are gender-responsive and assess the cost of gender mainstreaming in a flagship/regular program using the Harmonized GAD Guidelines pursuant to Item 6.4 of the JMC.
observations.managementAction: GAD code has been formulated. The GAD Code of the municipality has been formulated and a copy was submitted on March 29, 2016.
observations.reason: The GAD code has been formulated in 2014 and a copy was submitted on March 29, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer deposit all collections intact and as frequently as required to the authorized government depository bank to prevent risk of misuse or misapplication of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Balances were kept at a minimum.
observations.reason: Collections are still not deposited intact.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that concerned officials and employees submit justification on why the above excess travel allowances should not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Some ND justifications were received. No. 2016-001-101-(15) 2015 Municipal Mayor ordered immediate refund with options on the mode and amount of payment effective March 2017. Two officials have already refunded.
observations.reason: No justifications were received for some items. The Municipal Mayor ordered immediate refund; two officials have refunded.
